resolvedQuestions.label: Skill auto-loading patterns
resolvedQuestions.summary: Slim does install-time recommendation (presets) + runtime visibility filtering (filter-available-skills hook + getSkillPermissionsForAgent). No semantic auto-loading. <load_skills> is a task-call argument surfaced via retry guidance, not a plugin loader.

resolvedQuestions.label: Stack-agnostic detection
resolvedQuestions.summary: No plugin-level stack detector. CLI's package.json reads are about plugin location only. Reusable pattern is the codemap skill: agent inspects repo structure, infers include/exclude globs, persists hash-based folder map (stack-agnostic glob+hash plumbing).

resolvedQuestions.label: Caller-restriction enforcement
resolvedQuestions.summary: Layered: (1) OpenCode agent mode classification (orchestrator=primary, council=all, councillor=subagent+hidden, specialists=subagent); (2) per-agent permission map with deny-by-default for restricted tools (council_session denied for all except council); (3) plugin-owned runtime caller guard (council_session checks toolContext.agent==='council' and throws); (4) delegate-task-retry hook reacts to OpenCode core's 'Agent X is not allowed' errors with corrective guidance. SUBAGENT_DELEGATION_RULES is declared but unused.

resolvedQuestions.label: Write-capable safety guarantees
resolvedQuestions.summary: apply-patch hook is the strongest mechanism: parses patch, runs guardPatchTargets() to refuse paths outside root/worktree (throws 'apply_patch blocked'), conservative verification (missing update/delete targets fail; add/move refuses existing targets), preserves EOL/final-newline. post-file-tool-nudge appends prompt reminders. agent-mcps allowlist limits MCP surface per agent. Fixer is prompt-led (read before edit, no delegation, run validation). Default file-tool perms inherit OpenCode/user defaults.

resolvedQuestions.label: Sub-agent dispatch contracts
resolvedQuestions.summary: Two distinct dispatch paths: (1) OpenCode task tool with payload {description, prompt, subagent_type, task_id?} validated by task-session-manager (only when sessionAgentMap===orchestrator); (2) Direct client.session.create + client.session.prompt for council->councillor (passes parentID, agent, model, prompt, tools.task=false). SubagentDepthTracker enforces DEFAULT_MAX_SUBAGENT_DEPTH=3 (NOT strict depth-1). Multiplexer panes mirror parent-child via session.created events. Test invariants confirm councillor dispatch as raw client.session calls with abort cleanup.

keyFindings.label: Caller restriction = OpenCode agent mode classification
council_session has plugin-owned caller guard via toolContext.agent
council_session permission deny-by-default for non-council agents
SubagentDepthTracker default max depth = 3 (NOT strict depth-1)
apply-patch hook: root/worktree path guard + conservative verification + EOL preservation
Per-agent MCP allowlist with wildcard/exclusion semantics
Task dispatch payload shape {description, prompt, subagent_type, task_id?}
Council child sessions use direct client.session.create with parentID + tools.task=false
codemap skill is the stack-agnostic agentic pattern (glob/hash/diff workflow)
Plugin index.ts composes enforcement: registers agent/tool/mcp/config/event/tool.execute.before/chat hooks in fixed order
Orchestrator prompt explicitly documents delegation = child-session-blocking, parallel = independent branches; LEAF prompt should not have these affordances

ruledOutDirections.label: Schema-level caller restrictions
ruledOutDirections.reason: Schema rejects additionalProperties; no caller/restricted/dispatcher fields exist in agent override surface.

ruledOutDirections.label: Workspace marker file stack detection
ruledOutDirections.reason: CLI reads package.json for plugin location only; no go.mod/Cargo.toml/etc. probes anywhere.

ruledOutDirections.label: SUBAGENT_DELEGATION_RULES as enforcement source
ruledOutDirections.reason: Constant declared but no caller in repo; runtime enforcement is mode + permission + caller guard.
